headline: Shares of cruise companies are trading lower as crude prices jump amid uncertainty over whether the U.S. and Iran will hold peace talks before the ceasefire expires on April 22, raising the risk of renewed conflict.

headline: S&P 500 Eases On Iran Ceasefire Jitters, Crude Soars To $90: Stock Market Today
summary: U.S. stocks gave up early gains Tuesday as oil jumped nearly 3%. UnitedHealth surged 8% on strong earnings.

headline: Target Hospitality (TH) Soars 5.2%: Is Further Upside Left in the Stock?
summary: Target Hospitality (TH) saw its shares surge in the last session with trading volume being higher than average. The latest trend in earnings estimate revisions may not translate into further price increase in the near term.

headline: How The Royal Caribbean (RCL) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analyst Targets Reset
summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises now carries a slightly lower modeled fair value of US$348.46, down from US$362.04, a reduction of about US$13.58 in the latest update. This shift sits within a lively period of Street debate, where some major banks have been lifting targets into the US$370s while others have issued cuts as large as US$50. As you read on, you will see how these opposing calls fit together and how to keep track of the story as it evolves. Stay updated as the Fair Value for Royal...

headline: What Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL)'s AI Booking Push Means For Shareholders
summary: In recent days, Barclays highlighted Royal Caribbean Cruises as a leader in adopting artificial intelligence to streamline bookings and reduce dependence on third-party travel agents across the cruise industry. This emphasis on AI underscores how digital tools could reshape how guests discover, personalize, and purchase cruise experiences, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics among major operators. Next, we will examine how Royal Caribbean’s push into AI-enhanced operations interacts...
